description_long: This is a tall, slender clock tower with a rectangular base and a prominent clock face set into each side near the top. The structure is primarily constructed from light tan stone or masonry, with vertical grooves and subtle architectural detailing running up the shaft. The upper section features a decorative cornice and a white, multi-tiered spire roof with a pointed finial at the peak. The clock faces are circular, bordered with ornate trim, and display Roman numerals with black hour and minute hands. The overall design is reminiscent of the iconic Elizabeth Tower (Big Ben) in London, with a blend of Gothic Revival architectural elements and intricate stonework.
description: A tall, tan stone clock tower with ornate clock faces and a white spire roof, featuring Gothic Revival detailing and vertical grooves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main clock face, ornate trim, and the full height of the tower with its pointed spire.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, highlighting the clock face and vertical grooves, with the spire visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, displaying the clock face and architectural details on the shaft and roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other sides, with the clock face and spire clearly visible.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a decorative border, featuring black Roman numerals and two black hands (hour and minute). The face is set into a square frame with ornate trim, and the background is light-colored, possibly white or off-white. There are no additional decorative motifs within the clock face itself.
